subject: Product Management
title: Net Promoter Score
description: A tiny lesson with a big payoff: Net Promoter Score.

hook.text: Fred Reichheld asked one question: 'On a scale of 0-10, how likely are you to recommend us?' and built an empire on it.

buildup.text: Promoters (9-10) minus detractors (0-6) equals your Net Promoter Score. Positive is okay. Above 50 is excellent.

discovery.text: NPS correlates with growth because it measures willingness to stake your reputation on a recommendation.

twist.text: But NPS has critics. It reduces complex sentiment to one number and ignores context. A 7 means very different things.

climax.text: NPS works best as a trend indicator — track it monthly and investigate the 'why' behind the number.

punchline.text: One number can't capture everything, but the trend reveals a lot.

quiz.question: How is Net Promoter Score calculated?
options: Percentage of promoters (9-10) minus percentage of detractors (0-6)
Average of all scores on a 1-10 scale
